_readme: Second practice import pack — 11 problems across more patterns/difficulties (8 DSA + 3 SQL). POST to /api/admin/practice/problems with an admin bearer token (see seed/README-practice-seed.md). DSA = stdin->stdout judged via Piston; SQL = in-app sql.js judged against expectedColumns/expectedRows. SQL problems ask the solver to ALIAS aggregate columns so the column names match.

problems.title: Contains Duplicate
problems.statementHtml:
Given an array of n integers, print true if any value appears at least twice, else false.

Input
Line 1: n.
Line 2: n integers.

starters.code:
import sys
d = sys.stdin.read().split()
n = int(d[0]); arr = list(map(int, d[1:1+n]))
# TODO: print 'true' or 'false'

import java.util.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] g){Scanner s=new Scanner(System.in);int n=s.nextInt();long[] a=new long[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=s.nextLong();/* TODO */}}

hints.text: A hash set lets you detect a repeat in one pass.

problems.editorialHtml: Insert into a set; if an element is already present, return true. O(n).

solutions.code:
import sys
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]);a=d[1:1+n]
print('true' if len(set(a))<n else 'false')

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.statementHtml: Given a string s (no spaces), print the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

starters.code:
s = input().strip()
# TODO: print the answer

const s = require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8').trim();
// TODO: print the answer

import java.util.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] g){Scanner sc=new Scanner(System.in);String s=sc.next();/* TODO */}}

hints.text: Slide a window; track the last index of each character.
When you see a repeat, move the left edge past its previous position.

problems.editorialHtml: Sliding window with a last-seen map. O(n).

solutions.code:
s=input().strip()
last={}
start=best=0
for i,c in enumerate(s):
    if c in last and last[c]>=start: start=last[c]+1
    last[c]=i
    best=max(best,i-start+1)
print(best)

problems.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.statementHtml:
Given an array of n integers and a target k, print the number of contiguous subarrays whose sum equals k.

Input
Line 1: n and k.
Line 2: n integers.

starters.code:
import sys
d = sys.stdin.read().split()
n = int(d[0]); k = int(d[1]); arr = list(map(int, d[2:2+n]))
# TODO: print the count

import java.util.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] g){Scanner s=new Scanner(System.in);int n=s.nextInt();long k=s.nextLong();long[] a=new long[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=s.nextLong();/* TODO */}}

hints.text: Track running prefix sums in a hashmap of counts.
For each prefix sum p, add the count of (p - k) seen so far.

solutions.code:
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]);k=int(d[1]);a=list(map(int,d[2:2+n]))
cnt=defaultdict(int);cnt[0]=1;s=0;ans=0
for x in a:
    s+=x; ans+=cnt[s-k]; cnt[s]+=1
print(ans)

hints.text: XOR cancels equal pairs: a ^ a = 0.

problems.editorialHtml: XOR everything together; pairs cancel, the unique value remains. O(n), O(1) space.

solutions.code:
import sys
from functools import reduce
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]);a=list(map(int,d[1:1+n]))
print(reduce(lambda x,y:x^y,a,0))

starters.code:

hints.text: Sort descending and index, or keep a size-k min-heap.

problems.editorialHtml: A size-k min-heap gives O(n log k); sorting is O(n log n).

problems.title: Jump Game
problems.statementHtml:
Each element is the maximum jump length from that position. Starting at index 0, print true if you can reach the last index, else false.

Input
Line 1: n.
Line 2: n non-negative integers.

starters.code:
import sys
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]); arr=list(map(int,d[1:1+n]))
# TODO: print 'true' or 'false'

import java.util.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] g){Scanner s=new Scanner(System.in);int n=s.nextInt();long[] a=new long[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=s.nextLong();/* TODO */}}

hints.text: Track the farthest index reachable so far.

problems.editorialHtml: Greedy: keep the max reach; if an index exceeds reach, you're stuck. O(n).

solutions.code:
import sys
d=sys.stdin.read().split()
n=int(d[0]);a=list(map(int,d[1:1+n]))
reach=0
ok=True
for i in range(n):
    if i>reach: ok=False; break
    reach=max(reach,i+a[i])
print('true' if ok else 'false')

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.statementHtml:
Given a grid of 0 (water) and 1 (land), print the number of islands (groups of 1s connected horizontally/vertically).

Input
Line 1: R and C.
Next R lines: C space-separated 0/1 values.

starters.code:
import sys
data = sys.stdin.read().split('\n')
r, c = map(int, data[0].split())
grid = [list(map(int, data[1+i].split())) for i in range(r)]
# TODO: print the number of islands

import java.util.*;
public class Main{public static void main(String[] a){Scanner s=new Scanner(System.in);int r=s.nextInt(),c=s.nextInt();int[][] g=new int[r][c];for(int i=0;i<r;i++)for(int j=0;j<c;j++)g[i][j]=s.nextInt();/* TODO */}}

hints.text: Scan every cell; on an unvisited 1, flood-fill its whole island and count +1.
DFS/BFS marks visited land so you don't double-count.

problems.editorialHtml: Flood fill (DFS/BFS) from each unvisited land cell; count the number of fills. O(R·C).

solutions.code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(300000)
data=sys.stdin.read().split('\n')
r,c=map(int,data[0].split())
g=[list(map(int,data[1+i].split())) for i in range(r)]
def dfs(i,j):
    if i<0 or j<0 or i>=r or j>=c or g[i][j]==0: return
    g[i][j]=0
    dfs(i+1,j);dfs(i-1,j);dfs(i,j+1);dfs(i,j-1)
c2=0
for i in range(r):
    for j in range(c):
        if g[i][j]==1: c2+=1; dfs(i,j)
print(c2)

problems.title: Customers With Orders
problems.statementHtml: Tables customers(id, name) and orders(id, customer_id, amount). Return the name of every customer who has placed at least one order, ordered by name ascending. No duplicates.

sql.solutionSql: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id ORDER BY c.name;

hints.text: JOIN orders to customers, then DISTINCT + ORDER BY name.

problems.editorialHtml: SELECT DISTINCT c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id=c.id ORDER BY c.name;

problems.title: Total Revenue
problems.statementHtml: Table orders(id, customer_id, amount). Return the total of all order amounts as a single column aliased total.

sql.schemaSql:
CREATE TABLE orders (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INTEGER, amount INTEGER);
INSERT INTO orders (id, customer_id, amount) VALUES (1,1,50),(2,1,80),(3,2,30),(4,3,200);
sql.solutionSql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS total FROM orders;

hints.text: Use SUM() and alias the result AS total.

problems.title: Big Spenders
problems.statementHtml: Tables customers(id, name) and orders(id, customer_id, amount). Return name and total spent (aliased total) for every customer whose total order amount is greater than 100, ordered by total descending.

sql.solutionSql: SELECT c.name, SUM(o.amount) AS total FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING SUM(o.amount) > 100 ORDER BY total DESC;

hints.text: JOIN, GROUP BY the customer, then filter the aggregate with HAVING.
WHERE filters rows; HAVING filters groups (the SUM).

problems.editorialHtml: Join, group per customer, filter groups with HAVING SUM(amount) > 100, order by the total.
